([Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language"): *Giochi Olimpici estivi del 1960*), officially known as the **Games of the XVII Olympiad** ([Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language"): *Giochi della XVII Olimpiade*) and commonly known as **Rome 1960** ([Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language"): *Roma 1960*), were an international [multi-sport event](/wiki/Multi-sport_event "Multi-sport event") held from 25 August to 11 September 1960 in [Rome](/wiki/Rome "Rome"), Italy. Rome had previously been awarded the administration of the [1908 Summer Olympics](/wiki/1908_Summer_Olympics "1908 Summer Olympics"). However, following the eruption of [Mount Vesuvius](/wiki/Mount_Vesuvius "Mount Vesuvius") [in 1906](/wiki/Mount_Vesuvius#Eruptions_in_the_20th_century "Mount Vesuvius"), the city had no choice but to decline and pass the honour to [London](/wiki/1908_Summer_Olympics "1908 Summer Olympics"). The Soviet Union won the most gold and overall medals at the 1960 Games.

The **1960 Summer Olympics** ([Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language"): *Giochi Olimpici estivi del 1960*), officially known as the **Games of the XVII Olympiad** ([Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language"): *Giochi della XVII Olimpiade*) and commonly known as **Rome 1960** ([Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language"): *Roma 1960*), were an international [multi-sport event](/wiki/Multi-sport_event "Multi-sport event") held from 25 August to 11 September 1960 in [Rome](/wiki/Rome "Rome"), Italy. Rome had previously been awarded the administration of the [1908 Summer Olympics](/wiki/1908_Summer_Olympics "1908 Summer Olympics"). However, following the eruption of [Mount Vesuvius](/wiki/Mount_Vesuvius "Mount Vesuvius") [in 1906](/wiki/Mount_Vesuvius#Eruptions_in_the_20th_century "Mount Vesuvius"), the city had no choice but to decline and pass the honour to [London](/wiki/1908_Summer_Olympics "1908 Summer Olympics"). The Soviet Union won the most gold and overall medals at the 1960 Games.

The [1st Paralympic Games](/wiki/1960_Summer_Paralympics "1960 Summer Paralympics") were held in Rome in conjunction with the 1960 Summer Olympics, marking the first time such events coincided.

## Host city selection

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1960_Summer_Olympics&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Host city selection")]

On 15 June 1955, at the 50th [IOC Session](/wiki/List_of_IOC_meetings#IOC_Sessions "List of IOC meetings") in [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris"), [France](/wiki/Fourth_French_Republic "Fourth French Republic"), Rome won the right to host the 1960 Games, having beaten Brussels, Mexico City, Tokyo, Detroit, Budapest and finally Lausanne. Tokyo and Mexico City would subsequently host the proceeding [1964](/wiki/1964_Summer_Olympics "1964 Summer Olympics") and [1968 Summer Olympics](/wiki/1968_Summer_Olympics "1968 Summer Olympics") respectively.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

[Toronto](/wiki/Toronto "Toronto") was initially interested in the bidding, but was automatically removed from consideration when it failed to return the IOC's mandatory questionnaire by the deadline. The questionnaire may have been mislaid in the confusion following the death of the Toronto bid's chief organiser, [Robert Hood Saunders](/wiki/Robert_Hood_Saunders "Robert Hood Saunders"), in a plane crash weeks before the deadline.[[3]](#cite_note-3) This was the first of five unsuccessful attempts by Toronto to secure the Summer Olympics, the most recent being a bid for the [2008 Games](/wiki/2008_Summer_Olympics "2008 Summer Olympics").[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Highlights

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1960_Summer_Olympics&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Highlights")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b0/Torcia_Olimpica_Roma_1960.jpg/250px-Torcia_Olimpica_Roma_1960.jpg)](/wiki/File:Torcia_Olimpica_Roma_1960.jpg)

The Olympic Torch of Rome 1960

* [Swedish](/wiki/Sweden_at_the_Olympics "Sweden at the Olympics") [sprint canoeist](/wiki/Canoe_racing "Canoe racing") [Gert Fredriksson](/wiki/Gert_Fredriksson "Gert Fredriksson") won his sixth Olympic title.
* [Fencer](/wiki/Fencing_(sport) "Fencing (sport)") [Aladár Gerevich](/wiki/Alad%C3%A1r_Gerevich "Aladár Gerevich") of [Hungary](/wiki/Hungary_at_the_Olympics "Hungary at the Olympics") won his sixth consecutive gold medal in the team [sabre](/wiki/Sabre "Sabre") event.
* The [Japanese](/wiki/Japan_at_the_Olympics "Japan at the Olympics") men's gymnastics team won the first of five successive golds (see [1976 Summer Olympics](/wiki/1976_Summer_Olympics "1976 Summer Olympics")).
* The [United States men's national basketball team](/wiki/United_States_men%27s_national_basketball_team "United States men's national basketball team")—led by promising college players [Walt Bellamy](/wiki/Walt_Bellamy "Walt Bellamy"), [Jerry Lucas](/wiki/Jerry_Lucas "Jerry Lucas"), [Oscar Robertson](/wiki/Oscar_Robertson "Oscar Robertson") and [Jerry West](/wiki/Jerry_West "Jerry West")—captured its fifth straight Olympic gold medal.
* [Danish](/wiki/Denmark_at_the_Olympics "Denmark at the Olympics") sailor [Paul Elvstrøm](/wiki/Paul_Elvstr%C3%B8m "Paul Elvstrøm") won his fourth straight gold medal in the Finn class. Others to emulate his performance in an individual event are [Al Oerter](/wiki/Al_Oerter "Al Oerter"), [Carl Lewis](/wiki/Carl_Lewis "Carl Lewis"), [Michael Phelps](/wiki/Michael_Phelps "Michael Phelps"), [Kaori Icho](/wiki/Kaori_Icho "Kaori Icho"), [Mijaín López](/wiki/Mija%C3%ADn_L%C3%B3pez "Mijaín López") and, if the [Intercalated (Interspaced) Games of 1906](/wiki/1906_Summer_Olympics "1906 Summer Olympics") are included, [Ray Ewry](/wiki/Ray_Ewry "Ray Ewry").
* [German](/wiki/United_Team_of_Germany_at_the_Olympics "United Team of Germany at the Olympics") [Armin Hary](/wiki/Armin_Hary "Armin Hary") won the [100 metres](/wiki/Athletics_at_the_1960_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_100_metres "Athletics at the 1960 Summer Olympics – Men's 100 metres") in an Olympic record time of 10.2 seconds. American [Dave Sime](/wiki/Dave_Sime "Dave Sime") also ran 10.2 s in the final, but was credited with silver after a controversial video review.
* [Wilma Rudolph](/wiki/Wilma_Rudolph "Wilma Rudolph"), a former [polio](/wiki/Polio "Polio") patient, won three gold medals in sprint events on the track. She was acclaimed as "the fastest woman in the world".
* [Jeff Farrell](/wiki/Jeff_Farrell "Jeff Farrell") won two gold medals in swimming. He underwent an emergency [appendectomy](/wiki/Appendectomy "Appendectomy") six days before the Olympic Trials.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e8/Abebe_Bikila_sprints_away_from_Rhadi_Ben_Abdesselam.jpg/250px-Abebe_Bikila_sprints_away_from_Rhadi_Ben_Abdesselam.jpg)](/wiki/File:Abebe_Bikila_sprints_away_from_Rhadi_Ben_Abdesselam.jpg)

[Abebe Bikila](/wiki/Abebe_Bikila "Abebe Bikila") of Ethiopia wins the [marathon](/wiki/Athletics_at_the_1960_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_marathon "Athletics at the 1960 Summer Olympics – Men's marathon") barefooted

* [Abebe Bikila](/wiki/Abebe_Bikila "Abebe Bikila") of [Ethiopia](/wiki/Ethiopia_at_the_Olympics "Ethiopia at the Olympics") won the [marathon](/wiki/Marathon_(sport) "Marathon (sport)") barefooted to become the first [African](/wiki/Africa "Africa") and Ethiopian Olympic champion.
* 18-year-old Cassius Clay, later known as [Muhammad Ali](/wiki/Muhammad_Ali "Muhammad Ali"), won boxing's light-heavyweight gold medal. Ramon "Buddy" Carr was his coach.[[7]](#cite_note-7)
* [Herb Elliott](/wiki/Herb_Elliott "Herb Elliott") of [Australia](/wiki/Australia_at_the_Olympics "Australia at the Olympics") won the men's [1500 meters](/wiki/1500_meters "1500 meters") in one of the most dominating performances in Olympic history.
* [Rafer Johnson](/wiki/Rafer_Johnson "Rafer Johnson") defeated his rival, fellow [U.C.L.A.](/wiki/U.C.L.A. "U.C.L.A.") Bruin and friend [C.K. Yang](/wiki/C.K._Yang "C.K. Yang") in one of the greatest Decathlon events in Olympic history.[[8]](#cite_note-8)
* [Lance Larson](/wiki/Lance_Larson "Lance Larson") of the United States was [controversially](/wiki/Swimming_at_the_1960_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_100_metre_freestyle "Swimming at the 1960 Summer Olympics – Men's 100 metre freestyle") denied a 100 metres freestyle swimming gold, despite showing the best time.
* 16-years-old phenom [Chris von Saltza](/wiki/Chris_von_Saltza "Chris von Saltza") won four medals in women's swimming, three of them gold.
* [The future Constantine II](/wiki/Constantine_II_of_Greece "Constantine II of Greece"), last King of [Greece](/wiki/Greece "Greece") (abdicated and ended hybrid monarchy, 1973) won his country a gold in sailing: dragon class.
* The [Pakistani](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan") Men's [Field Hockey](/wiki/Field_hockey_at_the_Summer_Olympics "Field hockey at the Summer Olympics") team broke a run of [Indian](/wiki/India_at_the_Olympics "India at the Olympics") team victories since 1928, defeating India in the final and winning Pakistan's first Olympic gold medal.
* Wrestlers [Shelby Wilson](/wiki/Shelby_Wilson "Shelby Wilson"), and [Doug Blubaugh](/wiki/Doug_Blubaugh "Doug Blubaugh"), who wrestled together growing up, won gold medals in their respective weight classes.

### Lowlights

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1960_Summer_Olympics&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Lowlights")]

* [Danish](/wiki/Denmark_at_the_Olympics "Denmark at the Olympics") [cyclist](/wiki/Cycling "Cycling") [Knud Jensen](/wiki/Knud_Enemark_Jensen "Knud Enemark Jensen") collapsed during the 100km team race because of [heat stroke](/wiki/Heat_stroke "Heat stroke") and later died in the hospital. It was suspected that he had been under the influence of [Roniacol](/wiki/Roniacol "Roniacol"), a blood circulation stimulant.[[9]](#cite_note-9) The International Olympic Committee stated on its website that "drugs were implicated, although that was never proven."[[10]](#cite_note-10) It was the second time (and as of 2024, the most recent) an athlete died in competition at the Olympics, after the death of [Portuguese](/wiki/Portugal "Portugal") marathon runner [Francisco Lázaro](/wiki/Francisco_L%C3%A1zaro "Francisco Lázaro") at the [1912 Summer Olympics](/wiki/1912_Summer_Olympics "1912 Summer Olympics").[[11]](#cite_note-11)

### Historical landmarks

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1960_Summer_Olympics&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Historical landmarks")]

* [South Africa](/wiki/South_Africa_at_the_Olympics "South Africa at the Olympics") appeared in the Olympic arena for the last time under its [apartheid](/wiki/Apartheid "Apartheid") regime. It would not be allowed to return until 1992, by when [apartheid in sport](/wiki/Apartheid_in_sport "Apartheid in sport") was being abolished.
* [Singapore](/wiki/Singapore_at_the_Olympics "Singapore at the Olympics") competed for the first time under its own flag, which was to become its national flag after independence, as the British had granted it self-government a year earlier. [Tan Howe Liang](/wiki/Tan_Howe_Liang "Tan Howe Liang") won silver in the Weightlifting lightweight category, which was the first time (and the only time until 2008) that an athlete from Singapore won an Olympic medal.

### Non-medal winners

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1960_Summer_Olympics&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Non-medal winners")]

* [Finnish](/wiki/Finland_at_the_Olympics "Finland at the Olympics") [Vilho Ylönen](/wiki/Vilho_Yl%C3%B6nen "Vilho Ylönen"), a [field shooter](/wiki/Field_shooting "Field shooting"), shot a bullseye to a wrong target; in doing so, he dropped from second place to fourth.
* [Peter Camejo](/wiki/Peter_Camejo "Peter Camejo"), a 2004 [American](/wiki/United_States "United States") vice-presidential candidate for the [Green Party](/wiki/Green_Party_(United_States) "Green Party (United States)"), competed in yachting for [Venezuela](/wiki/Venezuela "Venezuela").
* The future [Queen Sofía of Spain](/wiki/Queen_Sof%C3%ADa_of_Spain "Queen Sofía of Spain") represented her native Greece in sailing events.

## Broadcasting

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1960_Summer_Olympics&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Broadcasting")]

* [CBS](/wiki/CBS "CBS") paid US$394,000 (equivalent to $3.19 million in 2024) for the exclusive right to broadcast the Games in the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States"). This was the first Summer Olympic games to be telecast in [North America](/wiki/North_America "North America"). In addition to CBS in the United States, the Olympics were telecast for the first time in [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada") (on [CBC Television](/wiki/CBC_Television "CBC Television")) and in [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico") (through the networks of [Telesistema Mexicano](/wiki/Televisa "Televisa")). Since television broadcast satellites were still two years into the future, CBS, CBC, and TSM shot and edited videotapes in Rome, fed the tapes to Paris where they were re-recorded onto other tapes which were then loaded onto jet planes to North America. Planes carrying the tapes landed at [Idlewild Airport](/wiki/John_F._Kennedy_Airport "John F. Kennedy Airport") in [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City"), where mobile units fed the tapes to CBS, to Toronto for the CBC, and to Mexico City for TSM. Despite this arrangement, many daytime events were broadcast in North America, especially on CBS and CBC, the same day they took place.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

### Participating National Olympic Committees

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1960_Summer_Olympics&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Participating National Olympic Committees")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/30/1960_Summer_Olympic_games_countries.png/250px-1960_Summer_Olympic_games_countries.png)](/wiki/File:1960_Summer_Olympic_games_countries.png)

Participants

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c8/1960_Summer_olympics_team_numbers.gif/250px-1960_Summer_olympics_team_numbers.gif)](/wiki/File:1960_Summer_olympics_team_numbers.gif)

Number of athletes per country

A total of 83 nations participated at the Rome Games. Athletes from [Morocco](/wiki/Morocco "Morocco"), [San Marino](/wiki/San_Marino "San Marino"), [Sudan](/wiki/Republic_of_Sudan_(1956%E2%80%931969) "Republic of Sudan (1956–1969)"), and [Tunisia](/wiki/Tunisia "Tunisia") competed at the Olympic Games for the first time.
Athletes from [Barbados](/wiki/Barbados "Barbados"), [Jamaica](/wiki/Colony_of_Jamaica "Colony of Jamaica") and [Trinidad and Tobago](/wiki/Trinidad_and_Tobago "Trinidad and Tobago") would represent the new ([British](/wiki/British_Empire "British Empire")) [West Indies Federation](/wiki/West_Indies_Federation "West Indies Federation"), competing as "Antilles", but this nation would only exist for this single Olympiad. Athletes from [Northern Rhodesia](/wiki/Northern_Rhodesia "Northern Rhodesia") and [Southern Rhodesia](/wiki/Southern_Rhodesia "Southern Rhodesia") competed under the [Rhodesia](/wiki/Rhodesia_at_the_1960_Summer_Olympics "Rhodesia at the 1960 Summer Olympics") name while representing the [Federation of Rhodesia and Nyasaland](/wiki/Federation_of_Rhodesia_and_Nyasaland "Federation of Rhodesia and Nyasaland"). Athletes from [East Germany](/wiki/East_Germany "East Germany") and [West Germany](/wiki/West_Germany "West Germany") would compete as the [United Team of Germany](/wiki/United_Team_of_Germany "United Team of Germany") from 1956 to 1964. Athletes from the [People's Republic of China](/wiki/People%27s_Republic_of_China "People's Republic of China") last competed at the [1952 Summer Games](/wiki/1952_Summer_Olympics "1952 Summer Olympics") but had since withdrawn from the Olympic movement due to a dispute with the [Republic of China](/wiki/Republic_of_China "Republic of China") over the right to represent China.[[13]](#cite_note-13) The number in parentheses indicates the number of participants that each country contributed.
